purpose: Provider-visible control packets with protocol-owned exact Claim boundaries and no gold support labels.

evidence.text: The deployment used a canary cohort. Only that cohort required rollback; the stable version remained available.

provider_packets.candidate_text: A canary rollout limited rollback to the affected cohort. This result does not establish behavior outside canary deployments.

atomic_claims.claim_text: A canary rollout limited rollback to the affected cohort.
This result does not establish behavior outside canary deployments.

evidence.text: Pilot team A used batches of 10 and received feedback the same day.
Pilot team B used batches of 12 and received feedback before the weekly review.

provider_packets.candidate_text: In the two pilot teams, smaller batches produced earlier feedback. This observation is limited to the tested pilot teams.

atomic_claims.claim_text: In the two pilot teams, smaller batches produced earlier feedback.
This observation is limited to the tested pilot teams.

evidence.text: A controlled test changed only the cache setting. Median latency fell from 180 ms to 95 ms when caching was enabled.

provider_packets.candidate_text: In this controlled test, enabling caching reduced median latency.

atomic_claims.claim_text: In this controlled test, enabling caching reduced median latency.

evidence.text: Two backward-compatible schema migrations completed with localized rollback and low recovery work.
An incompatible migration produced unreadable records and extensive recovery work.
The review protocol states that an incompatible migration with equally low recovery work would falsify the proposed compatibility pattern.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Backward-compatible schemas reduced recovery work in these migrations. The incompatible migration is a counterexample to the low-recovery pattern. A successful incompatible migration with equally low recovery work would falsify this pattern.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Backward-compatible schemas reduced recovery work in these migrations.
The incompatible migration is a counterexample to the low-recovery pattern.
A successful incompatible migration with equally low recovery work would falsify this pattern.

evidence.text: Two early-stage pilot teams obtained feedback faster after reducing batch size.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Smaller batches produced faster feedback in the two pilot teams. Smaller batches always improve feedback speed for every organization.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Smaller batches produced faster feedback in the two pilot teams.
Smaller batches always improve feedback speed for every organization.

evidence.text: High queue depth accompanied three observed latency spikes.
One latency spike occurred while queue depth was normal because a database lock stalled requests.

provider_packets.candidate_text: High queue depth accompanied several observed latency spikes. High queue depth caused every latency spike.

atomic_claims.claim_text: High queue depth accompanied several observed latency spikes.
High queue depth caused every latency spike.

evidence.text: In two releases, a compatibility check detected schema conflicts before deployment.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Compatibility checks detected conflicts in the two observed releases. The same check will prevent all future deployment failures.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Compatibility checks detected conflicts in the two observed releases.
The same check will prevent all future deployment failures.

evidence.text: Three support cases were resolved faster when logs included request identifiers.
The evidence does not record staffing levels or customer satisfaction.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Request identifiers accompanied faster resolution in three support cases. The improvement occurred because staffing doubled and customers became more satisfied.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Request identifiers accompanied faster resolution in three support cases.
The improvement occurred because staffing doubled and customers became more satisfied.

evidence.text: The large-batch release required a full rollback.
The small-batch release required only a localized rollback.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Larger release batches reduced rollback risk.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Larger release batches reduced rollback risk.

evidence.text: The experiment found no measurable relationship between reminder color and task completion.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Blue reminders increased task completion.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Blue reminders increased task completion.

evidence.text: Two teams improved after daily stand-ups.
A third team adopted daily stand-ups and its delivery time worsened.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Daily stand-ups improve delivery time in every team.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Daily stand-ups improve delivery time in every team.

evidence.text: The records describe database backup duration and restore success.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Remote work increases product creativity.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Remote work increases product creativity.

evidence.text: One query completed in 40 ms after the new index was enabled. No baseline or other query type was measured.

provider_packets.candidate_text: The new index improves all query types.

atomic_claims.claim_text: The new index improves all query types.

evidence.text: A team adopted a new planning template, but the outcome field was not recorded.

provider_packets.candidate_text: The planning template reduced delivery time.

atomic_claims.claim_text: The planning template reduced delivery time.

evidence.text: Two team members reported higher productivity at night.
Two other team members reported higher productivity in the morning.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Night work is more productive for this team.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Night work is more productive for this team.

evidence.text: The evidence records the server operating-system version but contains no energy measurements.

provider_packets.candidate_text: The server configuration reduces energy use.

atomic_claims.claim_text: The server configuration reduces energy use.

evidence.text: Pilot teams A, B, and C completed the task faster after the workflow change.
Pilot team D showed no measurable change in completion time.

provider_packets.candidate_text: The pilot teams completed the task faster after the workflow change.

atomic_claims.claim_text: The pilot teams completed the task faster after the workflow change.

evidence.text: Two early-stage startups in one accelerator reduced rework after weekly customer interviews.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Early-stage startups generally reduce rework through weekly customer interviews.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Early-stage startups generally reduce rework through weekly customer interviews.

evidence.text: Teams using smaller batches received feedback sooner, but those teams also had dedicated reviewers.

provider_packets.candidate_text: Smaller batch size caused the teams to receive feedback sooner.

atomic_claims.claim_text: Smaller batch size caused the teams to receive feedback sooner.

evidence.text: In two prior canary deployments, rollback remained isolated to the canary cohort.

provider_packets.candidate_text: The next canary deployment will keep any rollback isolated to the canary cohort.

atomic_claims.claim_text: The next canary deployment will keep any rollback isolated to the canary cohort.
